mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month employment growth (PAYEMS) signals robust labor market demand and consumer purchasing power, driving positive equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:January 2012 payroll growth at 135230k suggests ongoing labor market recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances where PAYEMS shows month-over-month growth, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 1.0% and remains in the lower bound regime, equity risk premiums compress and forward returns accelerate as investors rotate into equities seeking yield, producing positive 5-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.99% as of December 2011 indicates tightening cycle not yet at lower bound
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.3% or t-test p > 0.10), or mechanism fails to activate when rate is below threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, triggering negative equity returns as forward earnings expectations decline over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% and RSAFS declines > 3% over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1957k and RSAFS at 363806M as of November 2011
regime_hint: late-cycle demand weakness or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS decline simultaneously, or the interaction effect (joint decline) shows no statistically significant negative correlation with forward returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above its 12-month trend indicates expanding productive capacity and supply-side strength, supporting corporate profit margins and generating positive 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: When INDPRO 3-month growth > 2.0% annualized, SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean < 0.2%), or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative 60-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.99% and CPIAUCSL at 201.9 as of December 2011
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation concerns
failure_condition: When both FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and inflation > 2.5%, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, or the joint interaction shows no significant negative effect on forward returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, triggering negative equity returns as forward earnings expectations decline over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% as of December 2011 indicates relatively tight labor market
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession signal
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in 1 month, SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances, or mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration signals strengthening economic fundamentals and corporate earnings expansion, producing positive 60-day equity returns as investors reprice growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11403.6B (2017 dollars) as of Q3 2011
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth periods show no statistically significant higher average returns than negative growth periods (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth combined with housing starts (HOUST) expansion signals strong consumer demand and housing market recovery, jointly driving positive 5-day equity returns through improved forward earnings visibility.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 363806M and HOUST at 1957k as of November 2011
regime_hint: demand-side recovery or housing market stabilization
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST show simultaneous growth above thresholds, SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 45% of instances, or the interaction effect shows no significant positive correlation with forward returns (p > 0.05).
